title: Electride States and Superconductivity in Dense Potassium Carbides
authors: Jiance Sun, Ting Zhong, Shoutao Zhang
abstract: Carbon possesses remarkable physicochemical properties, enabling main group metals to react with it under high pressure and produce superconducting compounds. However, the research on high-pressure potassium carbides remains still unsystematic and inadequate. Using first-principles calculations and state-of-the-art swarm intelligence structure prediction techniques, we comprehensively explore the binary novel potassium-carbon system and characterize their structural, electronic, and superconducting properties under compression. Calculations of electron-phonon coupling show that monoclinic K7C (space group C2/m) with high K concentration is a zero-dimensional electride superconductor with a transition temperature Tc of 0.6 K at 25 GPa. By contrast, the Tc of orthorhombic KC with symmetry Imma increases upon decompression owing to the phonon softening of both acoustic and optical branches. Impressively, Imma KC reaches a maximum Tc of 21.4 K at 25 GPa, which is highly desirable for low-pressure metallic carbide superconductors. This work provides valuable insights into K-C compounds and broadens the diversity of metal carbide superconductors.
summary: This paper uses computational methods to predict new stable potassium-carbon compounds under high pressure. It identifies a specific phase of potassium carbide (Imma KC) that could serve as a promising superconductor at relatively low pressures (25 GPa) with a Tc of 21.4 K.
main_problem: The study aims to systematically investigate the structural stability, chemical bonding, and superconducting properties of the binary potassium-carbon (K-C) system under high pressure.
main_result: The researchers discovered several new stable K-C phases, most notably an orthorhombic Imma-KC phase that reaches a high superconducting transition temperature (Tc) of 21.4 K at 25 GPa.
method: The study uses the CALYPSO swarm intelligence technique for structure prediction combined with Density Functional Theory (DFT) and the Allen-Dynes modified McMillan equation for superconductivity calculations.
model_or_system: The physical system is a binary potassium-carbon (K-C) system under high pressure (up to 300 GPa), encompassing various stoichiometries such as K7C, KC, and KC3.
key_observables: Superconducting transition temperature (Tc), electron-phonon coupling constant (lambda), phonon dispersion curves, and electron localization function (ELF).
important_parameters: Pressure ranges from 1 atm to 300 GPa; Coulomb pseudopotential (mu*) set to 0.1.
main_assumptions: The study relies on the accuracy of the PBE functional within the GGA approximation and assumes the validity of the BCS theory for superconductivity.
figures_summary: Figures include electronic band structures, projected density of states (PDOS), phonon dispersion curves, and phonon projected density of states (PHDOS).
paper_structure: The paper introduces the research motivation, describes the computational methods (CALYPSO and DFT), presents the structural and thermodynamic stability results, analyzes the electronic and superconducting properties of specific phases, and concludes with the implications for metal carbide superconductors.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

(d) ELF of C2/m K2C at 25 GPa. (e) ELF of P63/mmc KC at 10 GPa. (f) ELF of Imma KC at 25 GPa. (g) ELF of P6/mmm KC2 at 100 GPa.
